mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p<0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 contradicting the negative direction over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which signals immediate economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during easing cycles, or the signal reverses direction with negative returns in >70% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.3% month-over-month forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of high-inflation trigger events, or the inflation-return correlation turns positive and statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels indicate stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings growth, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden contraction in supply-side activity and economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops >0.5% are followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.
